001.title: daemon command uses project_path for save_tasks but scan used brain.plan_tasks
001.failure_reason:
git apply failed: error: patch failed: grindbot/cli.py:430
error: grindbot/cli.py: patch does not apply

hypotheses.claim: executor.py is a high-contention file that frequently appears in multiple tasks per session, making it the most common source of merge conflicts
evidence: task-003 failed with merge conflict after task-005 merged first
hypotheses.suggested_action: The planner should treat executor.py edits as mutually exclusive and never schedule more than one task touching it per session

hypotheses.claim: Behavioral regressions in refactoring tasks originate from the orchestrator, not the executor - the executor faithfully implements incomplete instructions
evidence: task-004 was rejected because quote stripping was dropped and env var scope broadened, but the orchestrator instructions likely did not mention preserving those behaviors
task-001 placed a line before a None guard, suggesting the orchestrator's REPLACE block had the wrong ordering
hypotheses.suggested_action: Add a PRESERVE checklist to orchestrator prompts requiring explicit enumeration of behaviors that must survive the refactoring

hypotheses.claim: cli.py is likely being modified between when the orchestrator reads it and when the executor applies the patch, causing patch application failures
evidence: task-001 failed with 'patch does not apply' on cli.py:430
hypotheses.suggested_action: Investigate whether concurrent worktree operations or prior session merges are changing cli.py between plan time and execution time
